reforms.description: The Irish government aims to improve the innovation framework to enhance productivity growth and maintain international competitiveness.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing the innovation framework is expected to boost productivity and competitiveness, which are crucial for long-term economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has ambitious objectives for economic growth and social progress as set out in the 2007 Agreed Programme for Government.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in policy aimed at enhancing the overall innovation capacity of the economy, which is critical for sustaining growth.
reforms.source_quote: Further action is needed to strengthen competition to ensure that Ireland does not price itself out of the global market.

reforms.description: The Strategy for Science Technology and Innovation (SSTI) launched in June 2006 aims to double the number of PhD graduates in Ireland, addressing the need for a stronger domestic research base to enhance innovation capacity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing the number of skilled researchers will enhance the innovation capacity of Irish firms and contribute to productivity growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The SSTI was launched with the goal of doubling PhD graduates.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant structural change in the education and research landscape, aiming to build a robust pipeline of skilled researchers essential for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The Strategy for Science Technology and Innovation (SSTI) launched in June 2006 aims for a doubling in the number of PhD graduates.

reforms.description: The Strategic Research Clusters (SRCs) programme aims to bring together internationally-competitive researchers from academia and industry in key areas such as biotechnology, enhancing collaboration and innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering collaboration between academia and industry, the SRCs programme is expected to drive innovation and competitiveness in key sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The SRCs programme aims to bring together internationally-competitive researchers.
reforms.importance_rationale: This programme represents a significant investment in sectoral R&D, aiming to enhance Ireland's innovation capacity in biotechnology and other key areas.
reforms.source_quote: The Strategic Research Clusters (SRCs) programme aims to bring together internationally-competitive researchers from academia and industry in key areas such as biotechnology.

reforms.description: The Budget for 2008 allocated substantial funding for enterprise and innovation, aiming to enhance economic infrastructure and reduce social exclusion, while also increasing human capital.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for enterprise and innovation is expected to boost productivity and economic growth by enhancing infrastructure and human capital.
reforms.status_evidence: The Budget for 2008 included measures to allocate funding for enterprise and innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to enhancing the innovation ecosystem in Ireland, with potential long-term impacts on economic growth and competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: Substantial funding is also envisaged for enterprise and innovation, increasing human capital, and the social infrastructure.

reforms.description: The work permit scheme was changed to focus more on higher-skilled workers, limiting permits to a restricted list of jobs, thereby enhancing the quality of skills entering the labor market.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By attracting higher-skilled workers, the reform is expected to enhance productivity and innovation in the labor market.
reforms.status_evidence: The work permit scheme was changed to put more focus on the higher-skilled.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in immigration policy, targeting higher-skilled labor to meet economic needs, which is crucial for enhancing innovation capacity.
reforms.source_quote: The work permit scheme was changed to put more focus on the higher-skilled.

reforms.description: A 'green card' system was introduced for skilled workers, replacing previous work visa and authorization programs, aimed at attracting high-skilled labor to Ireland.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The introduction of the green card system is expected to improve the skill level of the workforce, thereby enhancing innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: A 'green card' system was introduced for skilled workers, replacing the work visa and work authorisation programmes.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it establishes a new framework for attracting skilled labor, which is essential for fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: A 'green card' system was introduced for skilled workers, replacing the work visa and work authorisation programmes.

reforms.description: Ireland has created the National Qualifications Authority of Ireland and developed the National Framework of Qualification to evaluate and recognize foreign qualifications, facilitating better integration of immigrants into the labor market.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving the recognition of foreign qualifications, the reform enhances the employability of immigrants, thereby increasing labor market participation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The National Qualifications Authority of Ireland has been established and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in how foreign qualifications are assessed and recognized, which is crucial for integrating immigrants into the workforce and enhancing overall economic productivity.
reforms.source_quote: Ireland has created the National Qualifications Authority of Ireland and developed the National Framework of Qualification since late 2003.
